source_intake_record.raw_question_text: Figure shows the deflecting plates of an ink-jet printer, with superimposed coordinate axes. An ink drop with a mass \( m = 1.3 \times 10^{-10}\ \mathrm{kg} \) and a negative charge of magnitude \( Q = 1.5 \times 10^{-13}\ \mathrm{C} \) enters the region between the plates, initially moving along the \( x \) axis with speed \( v_x = 18\ \mathrm{m/s} \). The length \( L \) of each plate is 1.6\ \mathrm{cm}. The plates are charged and thus produce an electric field at all points between them. Assume that field \( \vec{E} \) is downward directed, is uniform, and has a magnitude of \( 1.4 \times 10^6\ \mathrm{N/C} \). (The gravitational force on the drop is small relative to the electrostatic force acting on the drop and can be neglected.) What is the vertical deflection of the drop at the far edge of the plates?

image_dependency: Question explicitly references a figure and coordinate axes ('Figure shows the deflecting plates... with superimposed coordinate axes').
Upstream fields indicate requires_image=true and text_dominant=false.
However, the text itself provides all key numerical quantities (m, Q, vx, L, E) and the field direction, so the deflection can likely be computed without inspecting the image.
multi_step: Solving requires multiple linked physics steps: determine time between plates from L and vx, compute vertical acceleration from qE/m, then use kinematics to get vertical deflection.
The sign/direction of deflection also requires combining charge sign (negative) with the stated downward electric field.
This is more than direct lookup or single-step substitution.
verifiability: Answer format is a short numeric value with unit ('0.64 mm'), which is easy to check against a worked calculation.
Multiple-choice options are provided, enabling straightforward answer validation.
asset_registry_record shows question_present=true, answer_present=true, and registry_passed=true.

decision_reasons: question, image, and answer are all present with no registry issues
high verifiability and strong multi-step reasoning value support inclusion
image is referenced but core quantities are available in text, so the problem remains recoverable and usable
risk is limited to sign_convention_ambiguity and does not remove core semantic support
